seoTitle: How to Use Custom Fields in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to add, populate, and report on custom fields in QuickBooks Online so you can track the extra details your standard forms don't capture.
intro: Standard QuickBooks Online forms don't always capture everything your business needs to track, which is where custom fields come in. This guide covers how to add custom fields, fill them in on transactions, and use them in reports.

steps.heading: Open your account settings
steps.body: Go to the settings area where sales form content and custom fields are managed.

steps.heading: Add a new custom field
steps.body: Create a custom field, give it a clear label, and choose the transaction types it should appear on.

steps.heading: Populate the field on transactions
steps.body: Fill in the custom field data as you create invoices, estimates, or other forms it's enabled for.

steps.heading: Report on custom field data
steps.body: Use the field as a filter or column in relevant reports to pull insights tied to the extra data you've captured.

faq.question: How many custom fields can I add?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online limits the number of custom fields you can create, so plan which details matter most before adding them.

faq.question: Can custom fields be required on forms?
faq.answer: Yes, you can typically mark a custom field as required so it must be filled in before a form is saved.

faq.question: Will custom fields show on customer-facing invoices?
faq.answer: You can choose whether a custom field is internal only or visible to customers on printed or emailed forms.

faq.question: Can I filter reports by custom field values?
faq.answer: Yes, once populated, custom fields can typically be used as filters in relevant reports.
